mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135637.0 thousand as of May 2013
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.2%, the real policy rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and produces positive 5-day returns as market reprices duration risk.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 800 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 800 thousand units for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853.0 thousand as of April 2013
regime_hint: demand-constrained expansion with housing market weakness
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in > 50% of instances where HOUST < 800k for 3+ months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains above 7.5%, indicating supply-side weakness and labor market slack that constrains profit margins and produces negative 20-day equity returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with persistent labor slack and supply constraints
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO growth × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling strengthening consumer demand and forward earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations expand on improved growth visibility.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 421147.0 million as of May 2013
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in > 50% of months where RSAFS growth > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.15% combined with a decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%) signals monetary tightening into weakening demand, producing negative 60-day equity returns as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.15% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13746.2 billion as of Q1 2013
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization during demand deceleration
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month period, signaling accelerating labor market tightening and wage pressure that improves consumer purchasing power and produces positive 5-day equity returns through demand acceleration.
regime_hint: labor market recovery with tightening slack
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in > 50% of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 7.5%, indicating robust supply-side expansion with tightening labor markets that supports profit margin expansion and produces positive 60-day equity returns.
regime_hint: synchronized expansion with supply acceleration and labor tightening
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO growth × inverse UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
